title: blechnum erectum
intro: Blechnum erectum, also known as the erect chain fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is commonly found in shaded areas and along waterways. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been known to aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Blechnum erectum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
